page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 is a compute-focused server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 56 vCPUs mapped to 28 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading enabled, providing dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 224 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the x86_64 architecture and includes hardware support for AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, and FMA instructions. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark evaluations place this server in the top 10% for memory performance, as indicated by its PassMark memory score, and for text generation speeds on small language models. Multi-core processing capabilities are strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. The instance also demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations. However, single-core performance is more moderate, falling into the middle 50% for Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression. Large-block memory read and write bandwidth also register in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the c2d-standard-56 offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-efficient option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require specialized GPU acceleration or local NVMe storage. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This configuration is highly appropriate for multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, medium-scale LLM text generation, and parallelized image processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 is an x86_64 server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, featuring 56 dedicated vCPUs across 28 physical cores and 224 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show top-tier memory scores and strong multi-core execution, placing it in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, software compilation, and database operations. Conversely, single-core performance and large-block memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. This instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, offering qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized workloads. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 features 56 dedicated vCPUs and 224 GB RAM on AMD EPYC 7B13. It offers strong multi-core performance and top-tier memory speeds for database and compilation workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 is an AMD EPYC-powered instance with 56 vCPUs and 224 GB RAM, optimized for multi-core and database workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-free, multi-core x86_64 platform delivering balanced memory capacity and strong parallel processing capabilities for database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 56 dedicated vCPUs and 28 physical cores
224 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core benchmark results
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
Appropriate for database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded workloads
